# Plan - 2026-06-25

Source: `.github/research/2026-06-25-research.md`

The research surfaced exactly three actionable items, all from 06-24 /
06-25 upstream commits. No carryover items merited reopening (auto-
implement queue was empty at start of run). The three items are issued
in the order recommended by the brief: #2 first (stable input contract
on `agenticChat.ts:225`), then #1 (cache-preservation), then #3
(session revert).

## Issues created

| # | Issue | Title                                                                      | Category | Size           |
|---|-------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------|----------------|
| 1 | #847  | [tools] Normalize JSON-like tool-input strings against schema              | tools    | small (~3h)    |
| 2 | #848  | [core] Batch outdated-read rewrites to preserve provider prefix caches     | core     | small (~4h)    |
| 3 | #849  | [command] Session-wide snapshot and revert on top of file checkpoints      | command  | medium (~6h)   |

## Sequencing rationale

1. **#847 first** — it stabilises the `agenticChat.ts:225` input
   contract that #848 also touches. Coercing double-encoded array
   arguments is a pure pre-execute pass, so it cannot regress the
   prefix-cache work that follows.
2. **#848 second** — highest-ROI cost lever. Eager rewrites in
   `compaction.ts:165` invalidate Anthropic `cache_control` and
   DeepSeek auto-cache from the mutated message to tail; every turn
   pays full uncached input plus the 1.25x cache-write multiplier.
   Cline measured ~39% miss vs ~2% with batched rewrites. The
   `ALEXI_MIN_OUTDATED_REWRITE_BYTES` env knob (default 65536, literal
   `disable` for rollback) keeps this safely reversible.
3. **#849 last** — quality-of-life multi-file revert. Larger surface
   (new CLI subcommand + agent-loop hook + on-disk snapshots), but
   isolated from #847/#848. Files-only by design; messages are not
   touched. Reuses `FileCheckpoint.originalContent` so no new disk
   format.

## Validation against the codebase

- `src/core/agenticChat.ts` exists (788 lines); the
  `JSON.parse(toolCall.function.arguments)` site is at line 225 and
  the `tool.execute(args, context)` call is at line 241. Agent loop
  `while (iterations < maxIterations)` starts at line 516 — the
  snapshot hook target for #849.
- `src/core/compaction.ts` exists (491 lines); `pruneToolOutputs`
  starts at line 178, single-message size prune at line 157-173. The
  research brief's "line 165" claim maps to the in-place mutation
  branch inside `pruneMessageToolOutput`.
- `src/core/checkpoints.ts` exists (358 lines) with
  `FileCheckpoint.originalContent` (line 13) — bytes reused by #849.
- `src/core/streamingOrchestrator.ts` (197 lines) is the natural
  pre-provider request hook for #848 batched commit. The research
  brief's "settle hook" is implemented as a single `maybeCommit` call
  on the final assembled message array, not a generic event bus.
- `src/cli/commands/` already hosts subcommand-per-file modules;
  `revert.ts` slots in next to `chat.ts`, `sessions.ts`, etc.

## Carryover

- Auto-implement queue was empty before this run (0 open). After
  this run: 3 open (#847, #848, #849).
- 25 PRs merged in the past 7 days; engineering has bandwidth.
- #772 (demote claude-code) is NOT auto-implementable from this
  brief and was intentionally not requeued.

## Constraints respected

- 3 issues created (under cap of 5).
- Every issue body has `What`, `Why`, `How`, and `Done when` sections.
- Every `How` section names concrete file paths under `src/` and
  `tests/` that were verified to exist.
- No duplicate of any open `auto-implement` issue (queue was empty).
- All three issues carry the `auto-implement,enhancement` label pair.
